questions.question: Which of the following has pointed out that deciding on what is the best ethical course of action for people living in society is difficult?
Which of the following is NOT one of three motivations to act in the best interest of customers in the financial services industry?
Which one of the following is NOT a motivation for compliance with laws, rules and regulations?

options.text: Continue to be in the business.

questions.question:
Emma, a financial representative of an insurance company, would like to set up her own company to offer consultancy services to her clients so that she can bill her clients for the work she does, and not just on product sales. However, she has not sought permission from the insurance company she represents.

Which of the following statements about Emma’s action is TRUE?

options.text: Emma is ethical but not compliant.
Emma is unethical and not compliant.

questions.correct_answer: Emma is ethical but not compliant.

questions.question:
Christina is a professional financial adviser representative (FAR), and this role carries a privilege.

Which of the following statements regarding the meaning of this privilege is TRUE?

options.text: FAR has the privilege to be exempted from account opening fee when investing their monies with banks.
FAR has the privilege to be protected from being sued by clients.
FAR has the privilege to ask for sensitive financial information from a client.
FAR has the privilege to have special interest rates from bank deposits.

questions.correct_answer: FAR has the privilege to ask for sensitive financial information from a client.

questions.question:
Professional ethics is a set of code of conduct or moral principles on how a professional financial adviser representative deals with his various stakeholders.

Which of the following is NOT a stakeholder in this context?
Which one of the following violates the principle of professionalism in the financial services industry?

options.text: Providing guidance to interns and entrants in the industry.
Working collaboratively with other professionals in the industry.
Fostering a spirit of loyalty to fellow professionals in the industry.
Establishing a strict business and commercial position in the industry.

questions.correct_answer: Establishing a strict business and commercial position in the industry.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a duty and responsibility of financial adviser representatives in providing financial services to customers?

options.text: Follow the company’s code of ethics.
Sell suitable products to their customers.
Comply with the spirit and letter of the law.
Put the company’s interests ahead of the customer’s interests.

questions.correct_answer: Put the company’s interests ahead of the customer’s interests.

questions.question:
Natalie is a professional financial adviser representative and is bound by certain duties and responsibilities when recommending investment and complex insurance products to clients.

Which of the following is NOT one of such duties?

options.text: Obey the spirit and letter of the applicable law, rules, and regulations.
Follow the code of ethics of their respective professional association.
Put the clients’ best interests ahead of their own.
Guarantee the returns of the products purchased by the clients.

questions.correct_answer: Guarantee the returns of the products purchased by the clients.

questions.question:
Andrew is new to the financial planning industry. During the orientation training on branding, the trainer informed him about the four characteristics exhibited by a professional financial adviser.

Which of the following is NOT one of the characteristics?
Which of the following is the best definition of integrity?

options.text: Thinking free of bias and prejudice.
Keeping customer information in strict privacy.
Acting according to the same principles in every situation.

questions.correct_answer: Acting according to the same principles in every situation.

questions.question: John Doe is a representative of a licensed financial adviser. He was found to be in breach of the Personal Data Protection Act. Which of the following principle has John violated?
Which of the following violates the principle of diligence?

options.text: Performing work in a thorough manner.
Effectively supervising the people who report to you.
Being thorough and painstaking in your approach to work.
Setting unrealistic expectations about product performance.

questions.correct_answer: Setting unrealistic expectations about product performance.

questions.question:
Jana, a licensed financial adviser representative, always treats clients the way he would like to be treated himself.

Which of the following rules under the principle of fairness is Jana an example of?

Thomas is a successful agency leader who also serves as the President of the management committee for an industry association. He directs the association’s general manager to place the keyman insurance policy through his adviser, without disclosing this to the other members of the management committee.

Which of the following ethical issues can Thomas be held accountable for?

Esther is a licensed financial adviser representative. After successfully closing an insurance policy with Andrew, Esther promised an annual review with Andrew one year later. A year later, the review has still not happened because Esther feels that Andrew’s policy size is too small for her.

Under this scenario, which of the following ethical principles has Esther breached?

options.text: Principle of over delivering one’s promise.

questions.question: John Doe is a representative of a licensed financial adviser (“FA”). The FA is aggressively promoting a unit trust product (the “product”). John persuades his customers to terminate their existing insurance policies after a short period of time and use the funds to buy the product with no real benefit to the customers. Which of the following best describes John’s behaviour?

options.text: Cross-selling of financial services and products.
Churning and switching of investment products.
Excessive focus on sales target and commission.
Being practical in supporting the FA in its business.

questions.correct_answer: Churning and switching of investment products.

questions.question: Which of the following are obstacles to ethical decision-making?

options.text: Churning and switching of investment products.
Giving in to temptations and making rationalizations.

questions.correct_answer: Giving in to temptations and making rationalizations.

questions.question:
Jenny, a licensed financial adviser representative, attracts clients by offering them a large portion of her commission.

Which of the following statements regarding such ethical conduct is TRUE?

options.text: Rebating of commission to clients is an unethical behaviour.
Rebating of commission to clients is illegal and her license can be revoked.
Rebating of commission to clients is a criminal act punishable by a jail term.
Rebating of commission to clients is permissible if it is declared to MAS promptly.

questions.correct_answer: Rebating of commission to clients is an unethical behaviour.

questions.question:
As a licensed financial adviser representative, Clara faces four obstacles to ethical decision making.

Which of the following is NOT one of these obstacles?

options.text: Giving in to temptation on money.

questions.question: Which of the following will occur from time to time due to the complexity of financial institutions and the multi-functional nature of their businesses?
Which of the following is a manifestation of misaligned interest in the financial industry?
Which of the following exploits the information asymmetry between financial institutions and practitioners on the one end and customers on the other?
Which of the following regarding the suitability standard in managing conflict of interests is TRUE?

options.text: It is based on a set of rules.
It is based on a set of principles.
It is based on a set of guidelines.
It is based on a set of industry practices.

questions.correct_answer: It is based on a set of rules.

questions.question:
Peter resigns from his financial adviser representative role to become a banker. Using the data stored in his cloud device, he makes a cold call to all the clients who had purchased life insurance policies from him during his role as a financial adviser representative.

Which of the following ethical breaches has Peter committed?

options.text: Peter has misrepresented himself and may be subject to an act of fraud.
Peter has violated moral duty to his former clients.
Peter has committed unethical client-approaching technique to his clients.

questions.question:
Joe suggested to Eve that she surrender her existing traditional whole life policy and buy an investment linked policy from another insurance company solely to generate additional commission.

Which of the following terms describes this unethical behaviour?

Roger is a licensed financial adviser representative. His clients, Andy and Emma (husband and wife) have recently filed for divorce. Roger advises Andy to buy a large insurance policy for his elderly parents and park the policy under a living trust so that he can pay a lower divorce settlement to his ex-wife.

Which of the following statements correctly describes Roger’s ethical behaviour?

options.text: Roger creates a conflict-of-interest situation by putting Emma in a worse-off situation.
Roger finds himself in a “built-in” unethical situation.
Roger’s behaviour is ethical if he has declared the facts clearly to Roger’s parents.
Roger’s commission will be clawed back as such sales are considered unethical and not in the best interest for clients.

questions.correct_answer: Roger creates a conflict-of-interest situation by putting Emma in a worse-off situation.

questions.question:
David, a trustee, has a fiduciary relationship with Emma, a beneficiary. This relationship involves David discharging certain fiduciary duties to Emma.

Which of the following is NOT one of those duties?

You are a representative of a licensed financial adviser. You have heard customers telling you that they expect a “fair deal” in a business relationship with you (the “expectation”).

Which of the following best describes the expectation?
Which of the following is NOT a fundamental principle of fair dealing?

options.text: Timely and transparent disclosure of information.
Framing information to influence customer decision-making.

questions.correct_answer: Framing information to influence customer decision-making.

questions.question:
Joan has just taken a mortgage loan with ABC Bank to finance her investment property. The banker further advises Joan to place a fixed deposit with special interest rates from the same bank without letting her know that if she defaults on her loan, this fixed deposit can be used to off-set her liabilities with ABC Bank.

Which of the following breaches on fair dealing has the banker committed?

Clients’ interests are being protected via the five fair dealing standards by MAS to ensure fair treatment.

Which of the following is NOT one of these standards?

options.text: Clients have confidence dealing with financial institutions fairly.
Financial institutions offer suitable products and services for their target clients.
Financial institutions handle client complaints in an independent, effective and prompt manner.
Clients have assurance of guaranteed returns on their investment products.

questions.correct_answer: Clients have assurance of guaranteed returns on their investment products.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a step in the ethical sales process for financial products?

options.text: Use past success stories to gain customer confidence.

questions.correct_answer: Use past success stories to gain customer confidence.

questions.question: Which of the following is an ethical pitfall in the marketing and sales of financial products and services?

John Doe is a representative of a licensed financial adviser. He often fails to do what is reasonable in the circumstances in advising his customers.

Which of the following is John guilty of?

John Doe is a representative of a licensed financial adviser. He believes that prospective customers should be given any information relevant to their deciding whether to purchase financial products? Which of the following David Holley’s rules of disclosure strategies is John adopting?

Nethan, a professional financial adviser specialising in planned giving and estate planning, also works with a professional asset management firm. During a client onboarding and sale process, Nethan declares to the client that he will receive introducer fee from the professional asset management firm if he refers the client to the firm.

Which of the following statements is CORRECT about Nethan’s conflict of interest situation?

options.text: The sale can go through as Nethan has declared the conflict of interest to the client.
The sale cannot proceed because there is an ethical marketing issue.
The sale can go through if the matter is reported to MAS.
The sale cannot go through because it is illegal for an adviser to work with a professional asset management firm.

questions.correct_answer: The sale can go through as Nethan has declared the conflict of interest to the client.

questions.question:
Occasionally, financial advisers are overly innovative in marketing their products and services, and may fall into any of the 3 potential pitfalls in the marketing processes.

Which of the following pitfalls is an example of using “doomsday scenarios”?

Financial advisers need to be mindful of disclosing sufficient information to the clients and prospects.

Which of the following disclosure rules is recommended for financial advisers?

Mary, a client, needs to prove four conditions before seeking remedies for misrepresentation from Peter, an adviser.

Which of the following is one of these conditions?

options.text: The representation of fact was true.
The financial product fell short of the best-case scenario.
The client has suffered financial loss and damage.

questions.correct_answer: The client has suffered financial loss and damage.

questions.question: Which of the following expresses the view that customer’s service creates an opportunity to create a new relationship with a person and convert that person into a loyal customer?
Which of the following is NOT a benefit of providing good customer service in the financial industry?

options.text: Increasing customer choice of financial advisers
Reducing the time used to handle customer complaints

questions.correct_answer: Increasing customer choice of financial advisers

questions.question: Which of the following is used to convince others to do something that they really do not want to do?
Which of the following is a method to deal with challenging situations in the financial industry?

options.text: Using technical terms to convince customers.
Be empathic and apologetic for any inconvenience.

questions.correct_answer: Be empathic and apologetic for any inconvenience.

questions.question:
Persuasion is a critical communication skill in professional financial planning and is subtly different from coercion.

Which of the following statements regarding persuasion is TRUE?

options.text: Persuasion is an intentional verbal attempt to the client to do something he does not want to do.
Persuasion is an attempt to make the client decide on a product he does not desire.
Persuasion is an attempt to influence the behaviour of the client.
Persuasion encourages the client to think differently and buy larger quantum products.

questions.correct_answer: Persuasion is an attempt to influence the behaviour of the client.

questions.question:
John is a mid-career switcher to the financial planning industry, and one of the programs during the orientation is communications training. It is important to effectively explain relevant matters to clients in order to build trust and create a positive experience.

Which of the following is NOT relevant to effective explanation?

options.text: Adopt a state of professional preparedness.
Adopt a professional superior posture over the client.

questions.correct_answer: Adopt a professional superior posture over the client.

questions.question:
ABC financial adviser specialises in providing financial planning services to help clients rebuild their financial plans. It focuses on providing positive and uplifting experiences for clients.

Which of the following is a benefit of providing such an experience?

options.text: Increasing clients’ acceptance towards buying higher premium products.
Addressing simple complaints promptly by using Artificial Intelligence.
Enhancing the company’s image and reputation.

questions.correct_answer: Enhancing the company’s image and reputation.

questions.question:
Tense situations are inevitable while servicing clients over an extended time.

Which of the following is a recommended step in dealing with such situations?

options.text: Disregarding the situation entirely to avoid confrontation.
Avoiding potential liability by denying all inconveniences caused.
Offering financial compensation to the client.
Determining the facts of the situation.

questions.correct_answer: Determining the facts of the situation.

questions.question: John Doe is a senior representative of a licensed financial adviser (“FA”). An intern of the FA asks John a question regarding the prime objective of fact-finding in respect of financial planning for a customer. Which of the following would John’s answer be regarding the prime objective of fact-finding?

Jack Bean is the sole proprietor of an export-import business in textiles. He is told by one of his customers that a certain investment-linked life policy (the “ILP”) has the potential to make 12 % return per annum. Jack approaches you as his financial adviser representative to buy the ILP. Which of the following would you offer Jack?

options.text: A range of investment products with more than 12% return per annum.

questions.question: You are the senior representative of an exempt financial adviser (“FA”). During a skills training session, a management trainee asks you about the type(s) of insurance products where the guidelines under ‘The Life Insurance Form” is inapplicable? Which of the following would your answer be?

John Doe is a representative of an exempt financial adviser. He provides financial advisory services on life insurance. He is giving advice on unlisted specified investment products to a customer. Which of the following under section 4 (b) of the Life Insurance Advisory Form must be completed?

Jill Doe is an appointed representative of a life insurance company. She has a customer Jack Bean. Jack is 65 years of age, and he is illiterate in the English Language. Jill intends to sell a new investment-linked life policy to Jack, and she advises Jack to bring along someone to accompany him (the “accompaniment”). Which of the following is recommended with respect to the accompaniment?

Molly is new in the industry as a licensed financial adviser representative. On the first day of her orientation, she receives guidance that active listening is an important skill for a professional financial adviser.

Which of the following statements about active listening is TRUE?

options.text: Active listening is the process of focusing solely on the client’s verbal and non-verbal communication.
Active listening is the process of focusing solely on the client’s body language.
Active listening is the process of assuming the client’s unspoken concerns and desires.
Active listening is the process of assuming the client’s moral motivation behind his words.

questions.correct_answer: Active listening is the process of focusing solely on the client’s verbal and non-verbal communication.

questions.question:
A financial adviser needs to pay extra attention to the client’s liabilities position.

Which of the following statements is CORRECT if the client defaults on his liabilities?

options.text: Client may become bankrupt if the amount exceeds the client’s assets.
Client’s CPF OA can be borrowed against to pay the creditors.
Client will lose all his trust nominated insurance policies.
Client’s SRS will be terminated and the funds are transferred to his CPF.

questions.correct_answer: Client may become bankrupt if the amount exceeds the client’s assets.

questions.question:
Peter wants to buy a unit trust product and needs to undergo the “Client Knowledge Assessment” (CKA).

Which of the following options is NOT a CKA criteria?

options.text: Past Claim Experience against financial institutions.

questions.correct_answer: Past Claim Experience against financial institutions.

questions.question:
Peter is interviewing Mary, a client, for the onboarding process. He needs to collect information on Mary’s background as part of the mandatory “Know Your Client” (KYC) procedure.

Which of the following information is NOT required in this context?

Stephen, a licensed financial adviser representative, needs to comply with a series of procedures when recommending investment or insurance products to his clients.

Which of the following is NOT one of the mandatory processes?

options.text: Know your client’s sources of fund and wealth.
Complete documentation of needs analysis and recommendations.
Ensure client’s annual review is carried out.

questions.correct_answer: Ensure client’s annual review is carried out.

questions.question: You are a representative of an exempt financial adviser. You ask for the balance sheet of your customer Jack Beanstalk. Jack asks you for the reason for wanting his balance sheet. Which of the following is the reason that you want Jack’s balance sheet?
Which of the following is an example of your investment asset?

options.text: The expensive watch on your hand.
The stocks and bonds that you hold.
The luxurious penthouse you live in.
The savings and fixed deposits in your bank.

questions.correct_answer: The stocks and bonds that you hold.

questions.question: Which of the following shows how much a customer’s asset really belongs to him?
Which of the following statements regarding savings ratio is TRUE?

options.text: It is the savings (including investments) divided by net worth.
It is the savings (including investments) divided by the total income.
It is the savings (including investments) divided by the total movable and immovable assets.
It is the savings (including investments) divided by the total liabilities.

questions.correct_answer: It is the savings (including investments) divided by the total income.

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding liquidity ratio is TRUE?

options.text: It measures the number of months the client has in his CPF and investment portfolio to pay for his monthly expenses.
It measures the number of months the client has in his cash and cash equivalents to pay for his monthly expenses.
It measures the number of months the client has in his insurance and investment portfolio to pay for his monthly expenses.
It measures the number of months the client has in his SRS to pay for his monthly expenses.

questions.correct_answer: It measures the number of months the client has in his cash and cash equivalents to pay for his monthly expenses.

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding net worth is TRUE?

options.text: Net worth is total assets minus total liabilities.
Net worth is total income minus total cash outflow.
Net worth is the total sum of movable and immovable assets.
Net worth is the maximum future earning capacity of the client till his statutory retirement age.

questions.correct_answer: Net worth is total assets minus total liabilities.

questions.question: John Doe is a representative of an exempt financial adviser. He provides advisory services in the distribution and sale of insurance products, including investment-linked life policies. Which of the following should John recommend to customers under the principle of product recommendation of financial products?
Which of the following provides insured customers with additional coverage options and protection at reduced costs?

Jack Beanstalk is a retiree who had worked as a clerical officer with the Housing & Development Board. He has a GCE ‘O’ level education, and he has just celebrated his 70th birthday. John Doe, his financial adviser representative, considers him a vulnerable customer. Which of the following investment products would meet Jack’s savings needs?

Peter and Mary have just adopted a 15-year-old son, and they intend to buy a low premium life insurance policy to ensure protection for him.

Which of the following insurance products is a cost-effective way to achieve Peter and Mary’s objective?

options.text: Whole Life Insurance with Critical Illness Rider.
Term Insurance with Critical Illness Rider.

questions.correct_answer: Term Insurance with Critical Illness Rider.

questions.question:
Time value of money is an important concept in financial planning and communication with clients.

Which of the following statements about time value of money is TRUE?

options.text: S$10 today is worth much less than S$10 in the future.
S$10 today is worth more than S$10 in the future.
S$10 today is equal in value to S$10 in the future.
S$10 today is worth at least triple the value in the future.

questions.correct_answer: S$10 today is worth more than S$10 in the future.

questions.question:
Mary was told that a Will is an important document that she must have.

Which of the following statements regarding a Will is TRUE?

options.text: It is a document that sets out the client’s net estate distribution instructions after his death.
It is a document that sets out the client’s life insurance distribution instructions before his death.
It is a document that allows the client to appoint one or more people he trusts to act for him should he lose mental capacity during a terminal illness situation.
It is a document that allows the client to set out his wishes on dealing with his debtors.

questions.correct_answer: It is a document that sets out the client’s net estate distribution instructions after his death.

questions.question: A ‘Product Highlights Sheet’ highlights the key features and risks of an insurance product. Which of the following would have a Products Highlights Sheet?

Peter is a licensed financial adviser representative. He sold a retirement investment linked insurance policy to Mary and is required to provide her with the relevant materials and documents.

Which of the following is NOT part of the documents?

options.text: Illustration of past and future performance of designated investment products.

questions.question: John Doe is a representative of an exempt financial adviser. He provides financial advisory services to several types of customers. Which of the following customers would John find it necessary to review their financial positions at the end of each year?

options.text: Customers who are in the military service.
Customers who have tenure with their employers.
Customers who are entrepreneurs and self-employed.

questions.correct_answer: Customers who are entrepreneurs and self-employed.

questions.question:
Mary would like to terminate her term policy with her existing insurer and purchase a new term policy online.

Which of the following insurance clauses will UNLIKELY be re-set when a policy is terminated and replaced by a new policy?

MAS collaborated with the FA industry to produce a financial planning guide which can be used by the financial adviser to have a purposeful financial planning conversation with their clients. Which of the following does NOT support the purpose of the collaborated basic financial guide?

options.text: Includes all the insurers that are able to address financial protection needs.
Provides consistent guide for the financial advisory industry.
Includes a comprehensive set of rules of thumb for the key aspects of a financial plan.

questions.correct_answer: Includes all the insurers that are able to address financial protection needs.

questions.question: Which of the following is the recommended emergency funds purported by the basic financial planning guide?

options.text: One to three months’ worth of expenses
Three to six months’ worth of expenses
One to three months’ worth of income

questions.correct_answer: Three to six months’ worth of expenses

questions.question: Based on the basic financial planning guide under legacy planning, which of the following tools is NOT mentioned?

There are multiple tools for estate and legacy planning to address the effects of death, mental incapacity, and longevity risks.

Which of the following is NOT a tool for estate and legacy planning?

Peter wants to park some of his bonus as an emergency fund.

Which of the following instruments can he consider in achieving his objective?
Based on MAS’s basic financial planning guide, what is the recommended amount of death and total permanent disability coverage a person should have?

The Financial Advisers Act had consolidated three previous regulatory regimes (the “regimes”) governing the provision of financial advisory services in Singapore. Which of the following is NOT one the regimes?
Which of the following is the mission of the Monetary Authority of Singapore?

options.text: Regulate and control the financial sector.
Manage Singapore’s foreign reserves and assets.
Conduct surveillance of Singapore’s financial stability.

questions.question: Which of the following is an investment product under the Financial Advisers Act?

Peter is a provisional financial adviser representative and has not yet passed the requisite examination. Which of the following is the grace period for Peter to pass the requisite examination as stipulated under Notice No. FAA-N13?
In governing the financial advisers in Singapore, which of the following regarding the Financial Advisers Regulations is CORRECT?

options.text: It is an ethical reference for financial advisers.
It is a guideline for financial advisers.

questions.question: Which of the following is part of MAS’s (Monetary Authority of Singapore) mission?

options.text: Ensure retirement adequacy of retail customers.
Promote a sound and progressive financial services sector.
Promote high end financial products to all Singaporeans.
Manage the reserve for the Singapore government.

questions.correct_answer: Promote a sound and progressive financial services sector.

questions.question:
You, as a representative of a licensed financial adviser, have made statements to a customer during the sale process. However, you have omitted to disclose a matter which is material to the statements.

Which of the following regarding omission to disclose a matter material to the statements is TRUE?

A financial adviser is required under the law to have a basis when making a recommendation of a designated investment product to a customer (the “recommendation”).

Which one of the following is the correct basis for making the recommendation?

John Doe is a representative of a licensed financial adviser in life insurance. He tells his customers that life insurance broking premiums are kept in a separate account with a duly licensed financial institution.

Which of the following is where insurance moneys are kept and maintained?

The provision in section 45 of the Financial Advisers Act is to avoid conflict of interest situations.

Which of the following should a representative of a financial adviser do where such conflict of interests cannot be avoided?

options.text: Treat the customer fairly and equitably.
Treat the customer equally and professionally.
Introduce the customer to another financial adviser.
Refer the customer to seek independent professional advice.

questions.correct_answer: Treat the customer fairly and equitably.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a ‘director’ under Regulation 18(2) of the Financial Advisers Regulations in relation to the granting of unsecured loans and unsecured facilities to a director of a licensed financial adviser?

ABC is a licensed financial adviser, and it wishes to appoint John Doe as its Chief Executive Officer (the “appointment”).

Which of the following should ABC seek approval from for the appointment?
Which entity is empowered to make a prohibition order against any person barring them from engaging in any or all financial services regulated under the Financial Advisers Act?

options.text: Financial Industry Disputes Resolution Centre Ltd.
The High court of Republic of Singapore.

questions.question:
Peter is a representative of a licensed financial adviser. He has acquired some shareholding interest in a listed specified product (the “Interest”). He is required to enter the Interest in the Register of Interest in Listed Specified Products within a certain timeframe after the date he acquired the Interest.

Which of the following is the timeframe for Peter to register the Interest?

Any potential and actual conflict of interest in specified products (the “Products”) by the licensed financial adviser must be disclosed to clients in assisting them to make investment decisions regarding the products.

Which of the following types of decisions helps the customers decide whether to invest in the Products?

It is mandatory for a licensed financial adviser that receives insurance premiums from clients to establish and maintain a separate account with a duly licensed financial institution for its life insurance premiums.

Which of the following is the Financial Institution?

A licensed financial adviser (the “FA”) needs to provide recommendation on designated investment products to its clients under the Financial Advisers Act.

Which of the following is the CORRECT basis for making recommendations of investment products to the clients?

A licensed financial adviser omitted to disclose a material matter in its statement to a group of retail clients.

Which of the following relating to the omission in the statement is CORRECT?

A customer, Mary Jane, requests you as a representative of a licensed financial adviser to recommend her an unlisted specified investment product. Which of the following would you recommend to Mary Jane?

John Doe is a representative of a financial adviser in the life insurance business. He provides advisory services on life insurance and related products. He knows about the FAA-N16, which is the notice on recommendations on investment products to customers (the “Notice”).

Which of the following will the Notice be applicable in John’s advisory services to customers with respect to simple life policies sold as an ancillary product to loans with a simple payment basis for the insurance cover?

Which of the following is NOT involved in making recommendations on designated investment products to any customer under FAA-N16, which is the notice on recommendations on investment products to customers?

options.text: Conduct a probity check on your customer.
Document and keep proper records of your customer.

questions.correct_answer: Conduct a probity check on your customer.

questions.question:
You are a representative of a licensed financial adviser (the “FA”). Jack Bean is your retail customer. He has read the FA’s marketing materials about investing in unlisted specified investment products (“unlisted SIPs”). He is interested in investing in unlisted SIPs.

Which of the following should you conduct for Jack before you recommend an unlisted SIP to him?

John Doe is a representative of a licensed financial adviser (the “FA”). He is making recommendations in relation to overseas-listed investment products (the “products”) to a customer.

Which of the following must be given to the customer before making a recommendation on the products?

In recommending a customer to switch from an original designated investment product to a replacement investment product, which of the following will the relevant Authority consider whether the switch is beneficial to the customer?

options.text: Customer pays a small fee for terminating the original product.
Customer incurs transaction cost for terminating the original product.
Replacement product confers the same level of benefit at a lower cost.
Replacement product confers the same level of benefit at the same cost.

questions.correct_answer: Replacement product confers the same level of benefit at a lower cost.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a principle of general disclosure of information to customers under the FAA-Notice No 3?
A representative of a licensed financial adviser is required to disclose in writing to customers his status as a representative. Which of the following must be disclosed?
FAA-N11 is the notice on dual currency investments (the “Notice”). Which of following is where the provisions of the Notice apply?

ABC Financial Adviser has made the first recommendation to a client on an overseas-listed investment product. It is required to maintain the client’s acknowledgment of the risk warning statement for a certain period (“the Period”).

Which of the following refers to the Period?
A financial adviser makes a life policy recommendation to a client. Which of the following is NOT required to be furnished to the client?

Joe is a client of a licensed financial adviser (the “FA”). He chooses not to provide information to the financial adviser and proceeds with transactions in investment products independently because he believes that he understands the investment product based on his own reading on the internet.

Which of the following is ultimately responsible for Joe’s transactions?

A financial adviser representative must analyse the information provided by the client (the “Analysis”) to justify a product for the client.

Which of the following is the nature of the product from the Analysis?

A financial adviser can recommend a product in a listed specified investment product within the limited time based on the client account review. Which of the following is the expiry timeline of a customer account review from the date of the review?
For the purpose of conducting a customer account review, which of the following is the definition of a “Client”?
When a financial adviser is advising an unlisted specified investment product, which of the following procedures shall be conducted by the financial adviser?

A financial adviser making recommendations on investment products to clients shall act in accordance with the requirements set out in FAA-N16 (which is the notice on recommendations on investment products) in relation to certain aspects.

Which of the following is NOT an aspect under the requirements of the notice?

options.text: Providing financial advisory services to customers.
Informing customers about the investment product.
Advising customers on the minimum subscription amount, fees or charges.

questions.correct_answer: Providing financial advisory services to customers.

questions.question: Exempted persons are exempted from holding a financial adviser license in acting as a financial adviser in Singapore in giving advice concerning any designated investment products other than life policies to a certain number of accredited investors (the “investors”) on any occasion. Which of the following relating to the maximum number of the investors is correct?

ABC financial adviser appointed Ishikawa, a Japanese national, to be its provisional representative in the provision of financial advisory services to its Japanese customers located in Singapore. Which of the following is the grace period for Ishikawa to pass the relevant examination requirements to be a provisional representative?

Mohamed Ali, a Malaysian citizen, wishes to apply to be a provisional representative of an exempt financial adviser in Singapore. Which of the following is the minimum age entry requirement to be a provisional representative?

John Doe is a representative of a licensed financial adviser (the “FA”). The FA has reasons to suspect that John has committed acts of forgery and misappropriation of moneys (the “misconduct”). Which of the following should the FA do in reporting the misconduct?

options.text: Make a report to the Singapore Police Force.
Make a report to the Attorney General’s Chambers.
Make a report to the Monetary Authority of Singapore.
Make a report to the Accounting & Regulatory Authority of Singapore.

questions.correct_answer: Make a report to the Singapore Police Force.

questions.question: You are a senior manager of a licensed financial adviser (the “FA”). You are given the duty to make a misconduct report, if any, to the relevant authority. If there is no misconduct in the FA for a calendar year you are required to make an annual declaration after 31 December of that calendar year (the “declaration”). Which of the following is the timeframe for you to make the declaration?
Which one of the following is NOT set out under Notice FAA-N26 on Competency Requirements for Representatives of Financial Advisers?

options.text: minimum entry requirements for appointed representatives.
minimum CMFAS examination requirements for appointed representatives.
obligations of appointed representatives and provisional representatives of licensed financial advisers and exempt financial advisers.
obligations of licensed financial advisers and exempt financial advisers as principals of the appointed representatives.

questions.correct_answer: obligations of appointed representatives and provisional representatives of licensed financial advisers and exempt financial advisers.

questions.question: FAA-N20 is the notice on requirements for the remuneration framework for representatives and supervisors (the “balanced scorecard framework”) and independent sales audit unit. A representative is assigned a certain grade under the balanced scorecard framework consecutively for two calendar quarters immediately preceding the measurement quarter. Which of the following would NOT define the representative as a “selected representative” of the financial adviser?
Which one of the following is a member of the independent sales audit unit (“ISA Unit”) of a financial adviser (the “FA”) under the balanced scorecard and ISA Unit regime?

options.text: Head of sales of the FA.
Head of finance of the FA.
Supervisor of the representative of the FA.

questions.correct_answer: Head of finance of the FA.

questions.question: You are a member of the independent sales unit (ISA) of a licensed financial adviser. Which of the following regarding the number of rounds of post transaction checks should be conducted for each representative for every calendar quarter?

John Doe is a representative of a licensed financial adviser (the “FA”). His customer has made a complaint against John in relation to the quality of financial advisory services provided to him (the “infraction”). The FA in assessing the infraction found that the infraction impinges on the fitness and propriety of John as the representative. Which of the following category of infraction has John committed?

John Doe is a representative of a licensed financial adviser (the “FA”). He is assessed to be entitled to 70% of the specified income for a calendar quarter under the Representative Grading Table in its balanced scorecard framework. Which of the following is balanced scorecard grade given to John?

Jack Sparrow is the supervisor of representatives under his supervision. In a calendar year, the financial adviser assessed Jack to be entitled to 65% of total specified variable income from the representatives under his supervision. Which of the following is balanced scorecard grade given to Jack?
How many non-sales key performance indicators are there under the balanced scorecard and independent sales audit unit framework?

John Doe is a representative of an exempt financial adviser. He is required to comply with a non-sales key performance indicator (“KPI”) on the standards of professionalism and ethical conduct in relation to his provision of financial advisory services to customers under the balanced scorecard and independent sales audit unit framework? Which of the following is the KPI on the standards of professionalism and ethical conduct?

Peter is an authorized representative in arranging life insurance contracts, He needs to know the Minimum Supplementary Continuing Professional Development (CDP) hours requirement as he is new to the job. Which of the following answers is CORRECT?

To ensure the professionalism of the financial adviser representative, there is a minimum age for one to be appointed under 23(1)(a) of the Financial Advisers Act. Which of the following is the minimum age?

Mary is a representative of an exempt financial adviser. She receives 75% of specified variable income for the calendar quarter under the Balanced Scorecard Framework.

Which of the following is the balanced scorecard grade given to Mary?

A finding from a mystery shopping exercise determines that the quality of the financial advisory services provided by a representative to a client has a material impact on the interests of the client.

Which of the following is the category classification of infraction committed by the representative?
What is the maximum rounds of post-transactions must a financial adviser conduct for each representative in every calendar quarter under the Balanced Scorecard Framework?
What does the acronym “ISA” Unit under the Balanced Scorecard Framework in Notice FAA-20 refer to?

Under Notice FAA-N14, a financial adviser is required to lodge a Misconduct Report against its representatives for certain types of misconduct. The more serious misconduct will involve the police. Which of the following types of misconduct will require the financial adviser to lodge a police report and submit a copy of it to the relevant authority?

Peter is a proposed provisional representative and needs to have a minimum number of years of working experience relevant to the type of financial advisory service that he wishes to provide.

Which of the following is the minimum entry requirement regarding relevant working experience?

Peter is setting up a private client financial advisory firm serving accredited investors. Which of the following is the maximum number of accredited investors that a representative of an exempt person can provide financial advisory services to under Section 23 of the Financial Advisers Act?

ABC, a Financial Adviser, wishes to appoint John, an accountant, as corporate introducer. Which of the following statements regarding the appointment and use of John as an introducer by ABC is CORRECT?

options.text: John enters his name in the registry of representatives maintained by MAS.
John explains the adviser’s product to the client before handing over to the financial adviser’s representative.
John uses financial incentives to encourage the clients to buy policies from the financial advisers.
John enters into a written agreement setting out the terms of appointment.

questions.correct_answer: John enters into a written agreement setting out the terms of appointment.

questions.question:
Peter is a supervisor of 15 licensed financial adviser representatives under his unit. Last quarter, he obtained 50% to less than 75% of the total specified variable income from all representatives under his supervision.

Which of the following is the Balanced Scorecard Grade for Peter?

Notice No: FAA-N20 provides the regulatory requirements on Balanced Scorecard Framework and the independent sales audit units. And the term “infractions” is used to indicate failure to meet any of the non-sales key performance indicators by the representative.

How many categories of infractions are there under the Balanced Scorecard Framework?

A financial adviser is compelled under Notice FAA-N14 to lodge a misconduct report against its representatives for misconduct.

Which of the following types of misconduct is excluded and does NOT need to be lodged?

options.text: Failure to give cheapest product advice.
Failure to report representative’s current health status.
Failure to disclose to client about adviser’s credit rating information.
Failure to meet continuing professional education requirements.

questions.correct_answer: Failure to meet continuing professional education requirements.

questions.question:
Peter arrives in Singapore and is appointed as a provisional representative with his name submitted to the public register as a provisional representative. He will be obtaining the necessary qualifications to become a licensed representative in the meantime.

What is the validity period of Peter’s appointment from the date his name is submitted to the public register?

Financial advisers can appoint and use introducers, but they must comply with the requirements under Notice No: FAA-N02. Which of the following statements regarding the appointment and use of introducers is correct?

options.text: The introducer can make it a full-time occupation.
The introducer can use his own marketing script to execute his introducing activities.
The introducer can be a conduit to deposit the client’s money in relation to his carrying out of introducing activities.
The introducer must have a written agreement with the financial adviser in carrying out introducing activities.

questions.correct_answer: The introducer must have a written agreement with the financial adviser in carrying out introducing activities.

questions.question: Which of the following criminalizes corruption, drug trafficking and other serious offences?
Which of the following develops policies to combat money laundering?

options.text: Commercial Affairs Department of the Singapore Police Force.

questions.question: Which of the following regarding the period for the keeping of customer due diligence information relating to the business relations, business correspondences, and results of any analysis taken following the termination of such business relations is correct?

John has recently stepped down as the secretary-general of the Asia-Pacific Economic Development Secretariat, an international organization, where he held the post of secretary-general for the last 5 years. Which of the following due diligence would you perform on John and the Company?
Which of the following in the financial services industry must carry out on-going monitoring of a customer for the purpose of prevention of money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism?

A financial adviser is required to set up reference point(s) for the purpose of reporting transactions suspected of being connected with money laundering or terrorism financing. Which of the following in relation to the reference point(s) is/are correct?

options.text: A single reference point within the organization.
A single reference point within each unit of the organization.
A single reference point within each sales unit of the organization.
A single reference point within each compliance unit of the organization.

questions.correct_answer: A single reference point within the organization.

questions.question:
Peter has terminated the business relationship with ABC financial adviser, which has to keep records of customer due diligence information, account files, business correspondences and results of analysis (the “Records”) for a minimum period, for the purposes of preventing money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism.

Which of the following is the minimum period for the retention of the Records?

According to FAA-N06, a financial adviser is required to carry out enhanced due diligence measures on politically exposed clients.

How many types of politically exposed persons are there?

A financial adviser has a problem completing the customer due diligence measures as required under the law because the client is very evasive and uncooperative. The circumstances are suspicious enough to warrant the filing of a suspicious transactions report with the relevant authority (the “Authority”).

Which of the following authorities should the suspicious transaction report be filed with?

Which of the following monitoring is CORRECT?

In this context, which of the following individual customer’s particulars does NOT need to be verified for the purposes of preventing money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism?

options.text: Date of birth of the person.

questions.question:
A financial adviser has to set up policies and procedures to comply with MAS Notice No: FAA-N06, outlining the approach to prevent money laundering and counter the financing of terrorism.

Which of the following is the approach to be used by financial advisers?

There are specific regulations against financial misconduct in Singapore.

Which of the following is the applicable legislation for the prevention of money laundering for financial advisers in the conduct of their operations and business activities?

options.text: Corruption, Drug Trafficking and Other Serious Crimes (Confiscation of Benefits) Act.

questions.correct_answer: Corruption, Drug Trafficking and Other Serious Crimes (Confiscation of Benefits) Act.

questions.question: FAA-N17 is the notice on reporting suspicious activities and incidents of fraud by financial advisers. John Doe is the administrative manager of a licensed financial adviser who discovered an incident involving fraud (the “incident”). He is required under the notice to report the incident from the date of discovery. Which of the following regarding the reporting of the incident is correct?

FSM-N24 is the notice on cyber hygiene. Which of the following is the purpose of the cyber hygiene practice for the ‘network perimeter defence’?

options.text: To mitigate the risk of malware infection.
To restrict all unauthorised network traffic.
To conform to the set of security standards.
To access customer information through the internet.

questions.correct_answer: To restrict all unauthorised network traffic.

questions.question:
FSM-N24 is the notice on cyber hygiene, and it spells out the requirements for a “Committee of the Relevant Entity” (the “Committee”).

Which of the following is the minimum number of people in the Committee?

Which of the following defines DEF financial adviser?

Distribution of direct purchase insurance products are regulated by the Notice FAA-N19 (the “Notice”).

Which of the following is the Notice NOT applicable to?

The insurer must inform the relevant Authority within a certain time-period before the change is to take effect (the “time-period”). Which one of following is the time-period?

The fees should be calculated based on a fulcrum fee arrangement method.
The fees should be calculated based on a high-water mark arrangement method.

questions.correct_answer: The fees should be calculated and paid before consideration of all other payments.

options.text: Audit by way of the first method.
Audit by way of the second method.
Audit by way of the third method.
Audit by way of the fourth method.

questions.correct_answer: Audit by way of the second method.

Which of the following in relation to the insurer dealing in units of an investment-linked policy (ILP) sub-fund is CORRECT?

options.text: At least 1 dealing day a month.
At least 6 dealing days a month.
At least 9 dealing days a month.
At least 12 dealing days a month.

questions.correct_answer: At least 1 dealing day a month.

5 years working experience in the insurance business.

questions.correct_answer: 5 years working experience in the insurance business.

Which of the following criterion would John fail to satisfy under the ‘Fit And Proper Test’?

questions.question:

Stephen makes an application to be appointed as the Chief Executive Officer of the financial adviser corporation (the “Corporation”). He has more than 10 years of relevant experience in financial advisory services that the Corporation is licensed to provide.

Which of the following is the minimum years of experience in a managerial capacity for Stephen to be successful in his application to be appointed the Chief Executive Officer of the Corporation.

DEF Corporation needs to distribute life insurance online. It must hold a license if it wishes to engage in a business of arranging contracts of insurance regarding life insurance.

Which of the following is DEF Corporation required to hold?

Which of the following would the guideline be applicable?

Which of the following would you make the application for prior approval for the conduct of the roadshow?

John Doe is a provisional representative of an exempt financial adviser, and he sometimes advises his customers to switch from an original designated investment to a designated replacement investment product.

Which of the following is NOT one of John’s obligations in recommending customers to switch designated investment products?
Which of the following is the front-end monitoring of switching of designated investment products?

options.text: Customer declaration that he has been advised by his representative of the switch.
Procedures and controls to identify any unusual trends in switching transactions.
Checking the past transactions of customer for each new purchase to detect undeclared switches.
Checking the volume of switches to identify representatives with an usually high volume of switching transactions.

questions.correct_answer: Customer declaration that he has been advised by his representative of the switch.

questions.question:
FSG-G04 is the guideline to promote fair dealing when financial institutions conduct business with their customers (the “guideline).

How many fair dealing outcomes are set out in the guideline?

FSG-G04 sets out the guidelines to promote fair dealing when a financial institution conducts business with its customers.

Which of the following is responsible for charting the strategy to deliver the fair dealing outcomes to customers?

FAA-G14 sets out the guidelines on the balanced scorecard framework, reference checks and pre-transaction checks.

Which of the following non-sales key performance indicators has John committed?

options.text: Information about the client’s lasting power of attorney.

questions.question:

Which of the following is the Period?

Which of the following is the minimum number of closed sales that the supervisor is required to accompany the representative?

Which of the following should have oversight of the close supervision?

options.text: A person designated by the chief executive officer.

questions.question: The effective date in the case of an investment product (other than a life policy) is ______________________.

options.text: when the application is keyed into the product manufacturer’s online system.
when the application is received by the product manufacturer’s product team.
when the application is accepted by the product manufacturer.
when the application is delivered to the product manufacturer’s fund manager.

questions.correct_answer: when the application is accepted by the product manufacturer.

questions.question:
MAS has issued Guideline No. FSG-G04 to promote fair dealing by financial institutions when they conduct business with their customers.

Which of the following describes such a procedure?

options.text: Customer can have a 14-day free-look period.

questions.question:

Which of the following is the minimum corporate track record in the specific financial advisory services that it is proposing to effect under the arrangement?

The insurance company needs to conduct call-backs or surveys for all customers prospected at retailers and public places to ensure that customers have understood their purchases made at these locations.
