How many market conduct safeguards are there in ensuring adequate oversight of marketing and distribution activities at retailers and public places?

The board of directors of a financial institution needs to be aware of the Guideline No: FSG-G02 (the “Guideline”) which sets out the safeguards that financial institutions should put in place and adhere to when they market and sell financial products and services at retailers and public places (the “Market Conduct Safeguards”).

How many Market Conduct Safeguards are set out in the Guideline?

Portfolio drift occurs when natural shifts in the market change the asset allocation within the portfolio. Which of the following would address “portfolio drift” under Guideline No. CMG-G02 when the natural shift in the market alters the asset allocation within the portfolio?
Which of the following terms describes Robo-advisers?
Which of the following is one of the ASEAN participating countries in the ASEAN Capital Market Professional Mobility Framework?

ABC wishes to enter an arrangement with a Singapore entity to provide specified financial services (the “Arrangement”) under the ACMF Professional Mobility Framework (the “Framework”).

Which of the following is the minimum years of corporate track record that ABC must possess in the proposed specified financial advisory services in the assessment whether to approve the Arrangement under the Framework?

Before a client completes the purchase of a life policy via the online direct channel, the financial adviser should alert the client via a pop-up prompt or other device concerning the free-look period.

Which of the following correctly indicates the free-look period for life policy?

A financial institution advises others by issuing research reports concerning a designated collective investment scheme under the Financial Advisers Act. The financial institution and the entity offering the collective investment scheme are from the same company.
Which of the following could arise from such “Relationships”?

The revised code (the “code”) on collective investment schemes (the “schemes”) sets out the best practices on management, operation and the marketing of the schemes for managers and approved trustees. Which of the following best describes the nature of the code?
There were key changes made in the revised code (the “code”) on collective investment schemes. Which of the following is NOT a key change of the code?

options.text: Introduce principles on the naming of schemes.
Introduce a list of permissible investment for funds.
Introduce the concept of weighted portfolio maturities for money market funds.
Introduce the use of simulated past performance data for disclosure of performance figures.

questions.correct_answer: Introduce the use of simulated past performance data for disclosure of performance figures.

questions.question: There is going to be a change in the investment approach of a collective investment scheme (the “scheme”) in how the manager of the scheme selects investments for the portfolio of the scheme (the “change”). Which of the following relating to the giving of notification of the change to the existing participants of the scheme before the change is to take effect is correct?
Which of the following modifications of the trust deed under a collective investment scheme requires an extraordinary resolution of the participants?

In a collective investment scheme (the “scheme”), more than 10% of the scheme’s net asset value is sub-managed abroad. The manager should, together with its related corporations, already be managing at least a certain dollar value of discretionary funds in Singapore. Which of the following is the minimum dollar value of discretionary funds that should already be managed in Singapore?
Which of the following relating to the period over which performance fee should accrue and the frequency of crystallization on a collective investment scheme is correct?
Which of the following is the arrangement to calculate the performance fee of a collective investment scheme, where it is a percentage of the net asset value of its base fee?

The underlying assets of a collective investment scheme may only consist of permissible investments as set out under the revised code on investment schemes.

Which of the following is NOT an underlying investment permitted under the code?
Which of the following is NOT considered a collateral for the purpose of securities lending and repurchase transactions under the revised code on collective investment schemes?

A collective investment scheme (the “scheme”) may borrow on a temporary basis for the purpose of meeting redemption requirements (the “borrowing”).

Which of the following is the maximum amount that the scheme can borrow in relation to its net asset value at the time of borrowing?

There are 5 exceptions arising from the back-testing outcomes in calculating the global exposure to the collective investment scheme, and color zones are used to assess the back-testing outcomes of the scheme.

Which of the following color zones is used to assess the back-testing outcomes of the 5 exceptions?

ABC insurer runs a collective investment scheme. It needs temporary liquidity to meet a redemption obligation, and may borrow from a licensed bank under the Banking Act on a temporary basis.

Which of the following in relation to the aggregate borrowing for the Purposes at the time the borrowing is incurred is CORRECT?

options.text: At maximum 10% of the scheme’s net asset value.
At maximum 20% of the scheme’s net asset value.
At maximum 30% of the scheme’s net asset value.
At maximum 40% of the scheme’s net asset value.

questions.correct_answer: At maximum 10% of the scheme’s net asset value.

questions.question: A manager has several approaches to calculate the global exposure of a collective investment scheme to financial derivatives. How many approaches are there?

ABC bank is considering designing a Collective investment scheme (CIS) to be sold to their deposit holders. The CIS may only consist of permissible investments.

Which of the following is NOT a permissible investment?

A manager of a collective investment scheme needs to set up a policy to deal with the units in the CIS.
Which of the following in relation to the manager dealing in units in a collective investment scheme is CORRECT?

options.text: At least 1 dealing day a month.
At least 5 dealings days a month.
At least 7 dealing days a month.
At least 14 dealing days a month.

questions.correct_answer: At least 1 dealing day a month.

questions.question:
John is proposing a performance fee to the board of directors for approval for a new collective investment scheme.
Which of the following should the proposal use as a basis of calculation?

Which of the following should the manager in Singapore, together with its related corporations, already be managing in terms of discretionary funds in Singapore, given that the collective investment scheme’s net asset value (NAV) is invested in other schemes registered under Hongkong jurisdiction?

ABC Trustee needs to notify the relevant authority on the maturity of a collective investment scheme with a fixed maturity date under the revised Code on Collective Scheme.
Which of the following statements is CORRECT?

options.text: Within 2 weeks of the maturity date of the scheme.
Within 8 weeks of the maturity date of the scheme.
Within 16 weeks of the maturity date of the scheme.
Within 24 weeks of the maturity date of the scheme.

questions.correct_answer: Within 2 weeks of the maturity date of the scheme.

questions.question:
ABC fund manager wants to strengthen the core investment requirement for collective investment schemes under the revised Code on Collective Investment Schemes.
Which of the following statements is CORRECT?

options.text: Enhancing guidelines on securities lending activities.
Establishing new guidelines for derivatives used inside the funds.
Due diligent research on the assets under management.
Installing standards to equalised performance fees.

questions.correct_answer: Enhancing guidelines on securities lending activities.

questions.question:
The Code on Collective Investment Schemes issued pursuant to section 321 of the Securities and Futures Act needs to be understood clearly.
Which of the following statements is CORRECT?

options.text: It is civil law in nature.

questions.question:
John operates in Hong Kong as a dealer in securities. He carries out transactions intending to manipulate prices of penny stocks quotes on a Singapore-based exchange (“acts of price manipulation”). The acts of price manipulation are market misconduct offences.

Which one of following relating to market misconduct offences is NOT true?

options.text: John will face the penalty of a fine.
John will face the penalty of imprisonment.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a prohibited market conduct under the Securities and Futures Act (SFA)?
John uses artificial methods to cause price volatility of a stock in the securities market. Which one of the following prohibited market conducts has John committed?

options.text: false trading and market rigging transactions.
false and misleading statements and information.

questions.correct_answer: false trading and market rigging transactions.

questions.question: Which one of the following refers to the buying and selling of equity shares without a change in the beneficial ownership?
Which one of the following refers to the intentional interference with the forces of supply and demand to defraud investors in the capital market?

options.text: employment of manipulate and deceptive devices.
fraudulently inducing person to deal in capital market products.

questions.question: John makes a material statement he knows to be false in connection with the subscription of a capital markets product. Which one of the following market misconducts has John committed?

options.text: false trading and market rigging transactions.
employment of manipulative and deceptive devices.

questions.correct_answer: employment of manipulative and deceptive devices.

questions.question: John walks into a bank and enquires about obtaining a credit card. When he is waiting for his credit card operation to be processed, a bank officer approaches him to sell a collective investment scheme unit (CIS) to him. John says he is not really interested but the bank officer continues with the sales pitch, coaxing John to consider investing in the CIS units. Which one of the following relates to the above fact situation?
Short selling is defined as the sale of specified capital markets products that the seller does not have at the time of the sale. How many types of short selling are there?

John will be inheriting substantial shares from his deceased father’s estate in 2 weeks’ time. However, he is selling the shares today to Mary to raise liquidity to clear the deceased father’s estate liabilities and will deliver the shares to Mary in 2 weeks. John has already arranged to meet his delivery obligation although he does not own the shares yet.

Which of the following describes John’s conduct in the share market?

Eve is a private banker of Peter who is a director of a mainboard listed company, ABC Limited (“ABC”). Peter passes an order to Eve to sell the bulk of his personal shares in ABC. Although this trade information has not been made public, Eve shared this information with her husband, Andy.

Which of the following market conduct has Eve breached?

Joan has just received her critical illness insurance payout due to her cancer situation. she visits a bank to deposit her critical illness insurance proceeds. The teller informs the relationship manager (RM) about Joan, and subsequently, the RM approaches Joan to invest her insurance proceeds in securities-based derivative contracts without first performing a financial need analysis.

Which of the following refers to the market conduct of the relationship manager of the bank?

Adam needs to implement a corporate governance policy to prevent the board of directors who are “in the know” from taking advantage of and benefiting from the resultant market movements in capital markets products.

Which of the following rules is Adam implementing?

John is a representative of CMS license holder. During a company gala dinner event, he overstated certain details relating to the subscription of securities-based derivative contracts which led some event participants to subscribe based on inaccurate information.

Which of the following market misconducts has John breached?

options.text: False securities and futures market rigging.

questions.question:
Peter executes a buy order using his account and his family members accounts to buy his company’s shares above previous trade price to boost the price of the shares ahead of the company’s AGM.

Which of the following market misconduct has Peter breached?

Mary is under investigation for a “wash sale” where she conducted a share trading without changing the beneficial ownership.

Which of the following market misconduct is a “wash sale”?

options.text: False trading and market rigging transactions.

questions.correct_answer: False trading and market rigging transactions.

questions.question:
Peter just took on a C-suite role of an FA firm. He needs to be briefed on the types of prohibited market conduct for all participants of capital markets (“Market Misconduct”) under the Securities and Futures Act (SFA).

How many types of prohibited Market Misconduct are set out under the SFA?
Which of the following CPF accounts can be used for the down payment of a residential property?
From 2016, which of the following plans are NOT offered under CPF LIFE scheme?
Which of the following statements about CareShield Life is FALSE?

options.text: CareShield Life premiums can be fully payable by Medisave.
CareShield Life allows for payouts to increase over time until age 67.
CareShield Life provides lifetime cash payouts for as long as one is severely disabled.
CareShield Life is previously known as MediShield Life.

questions.correct_answer: CareShield Life is previously known as MediShield Life.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Dependants’ Protection Scheme (DPS) is TRUE?

options.text: DPS is a mortgage reducing term insurance.
DPS is administered by all life insurers in Singapore.
DPS is compulsory for all CPF members age 21 and above.
DPS benefit will be paid out if the CPF member passes away.

questions.correct_answer: DPS benefit will be paid out if the CPF member passes away.

questions.question: A CPF member may use his Medisave savings to buy Medisave approved Integrated Shield Plans for his:
Which of the following was the original objective of the Central Provident Fund when it first started on 1 July 1955?
Which of the following statements relating to the Supplementary Retirement Scheme (SRS) is CORRECT?

options.text: The SRS is a mandatory scheme that complements the CPF.
SRS withdrawals are subject to withdrawal charges under all circumstances.
An SRS participant is not allowed to buy into the same investment that he has previously purchased.
An SRS participant can choose the amount to contribute to SRS, subject to a cap.

questions.correct_answer: An SRS participant can choose the amount to contribute to SRS, subject to a cap.

questions.question: Under CareShield Life, the eligible CPF member will receive a monthly benefit:
Under the CPF Investment Scheme (CPFIS), which of the following statements is NOT CORRECT?

options.text: The CPFIS aims to allow CPF members to potentially enhance their retirement savings.
CPF Members are allowed to invest in Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) under the CPFIS.
CPF Members are required to have a certain amount in the CPF Ordinary Account before participating in CPFIS.
New CPFIS Investors are required to take the Customer Knowledge Assessment (CKA) on the CPF website before investing in specialised funds.

questions.correct_answer: New CPFIS Investors are required to take the Customer Knowledge Assessment (CKA) on the CPF website before investing in specialised funds.

questions.question: A CPF member who wants to participate in the CPF Investment Scheme (CPFIS) must be at least what age?

Joe lost a lawsuit and is liable to pay $2m to the claimant. He does not have enough bankable assets to pay for the damage. However, he and his wife, Mary, have a fully paid private property. Joe is thinking of taking up home equity loans to pay his legal damages.
Which of the following statements correctly spells out the risk of this strategy?

options.text: Joe and Mary should consider speaking with the financial institutions to understand the risk as they may lose the property if they are unable to pay back the loan.
Home equity loan is not allowed on private property owned by an individual who is currently undergoing a lawsuit and is subject to paying damages.
There is a 30% stamp duty on the property value to be imposed on the home equity loan as it is considered an ownership of a second property.
Joe and Mary cannot sell their private property until after 5 years of waiting period from the issuance of the home equity loan.

questions.correct_answer: Joe and Mary should consider speaking with the financial institutions to understand the risk as they may lose the property if they are unable to pay back the loan.

questions.question:
Eve is considering buying a HDB flat and is seeking a mortgage loan from a bank.
Which of the following documents does Eve need to have before she can exercise the Option to Purchase for her desired property?

options.text: Letter of Offer from the bank.
Letter of discharge from the bank.
Letter of approval from the bank.
Letter of consent to right of set off from the bank.

questions.correct_answer: Letter of Offer from the bank.

questions.question:
Besides retirement, CPF can be used for other objectives such as property purchases. Which of the following statements correctly describes the funding mechanism of CPF for its members in their purchases of properties?
OA = Ordinary Account
SA = Special Account
RA = Retirement Account

options.text: CPF members can use their CPF- OA, SA & RA to fund their purchases of properties.
CPF members can only use their CPFOA to fund their purchases of properties.
CPF members can only use their CPF after they have met 150% of the Basic Retirement Sum (BRS) amount to fund their purchases of properties.
CPF members can only use their CPF after they have met 150% of Full Retirement Sum (FRS) amount to fund their purchases of properties.

questions.correct_answer: CPF members can only use their CPFOA to fund their purchases of properties.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the limitation of Eldershield compared to CareShield Life?

options.text: Payout from ElderShield is of a limited period whilst payout from CareShield Life can be for lifetime (assuming the CPF member does not recover from severe disability).
Premium from ElderShield is not tax deductible, whilst premium for CareShield Life is tax deductible.
ElderShield is managed by private insurers and hence the payout is more stringent than CareShield Life, which is managed by CPF Board.
Payouts from ElderShield is not creditor protected whilst payout from CareShield Life is creditor protected.

questions.correct_answer: Payout from ElderShield is of a limited period whilst payout from CareShield Life can be for lifetime (assuming the CPF member does not recover from severe disability).

questions.question:
John is 75 years old. He has a basic Integrated Shield plan which can be partially funded by the CPF Medisave account, subject to additional withdrawal limits (AWLs),
Given John’s age, which of the following AWL amount is applicable to him?
Serene is investing part of her CPF OA for her retirement. Her current CPF OA balance is $50,000. What is the minimum amount she needs to have in her CPF OA balance before investing?

Additional simple interests are credited to the CPF members’ accounts to boost the members’ savings. For CPF members who are below age 55, which of the following statements regarding additional simple interest is correct?
OA = Ordinary Account
SA = Special Account
RA = Retirement Account

options.text: Members earn an extra interest of 3% per annum on the first $30K (capped at $20K for OA) and 2% per annum on the next $30K of combined CPF balances.
Members earn an extra interest of 1% per annum on the first $60K of combined CPF balances (capped at $20K for OA).
Members earn an extra interest of 1% per annum on the first $50K of the combined CPF balances (capped at $10K for OA).
Members earn an extra interest of 1% per annum on the first $100K (capped at $30K for OA) of combined CPF OA, SA and RA balances.

questions.correct_answer: Members earn an extra interest of 1% per annum on the first $60K of combined CPF balances (capped at $20K for OA).

questions.question:
John has just acquired Singapore permanent residency and is now a CPF member. He wants to understand the returns from CPF Special Account.
Which of the following regarding the return from CPF Special Account is CORRECT?

options.text: CPF Special Account is based on the 3-month average of major local banks’ interest rates, subject to a legislated minimum interest of 4% per annum.
CPF Special Account is based on the 3-month average of major local banks’ interest rates, subject to a legislated minimum interest of 6% per annum.
CPF Special Account is based on the 12-month average yield of the 15-year MAS Securities plus 1%, subject to the current floor interest rate of 4% per annum.
CPF Special Account is based on the 12-month average yield of the 10-year Singapore Government Securities (SGS) plus 1%, subject to the current floor interest rate of 4% per annum.

questions.correct_answer: CPF Special Account is based on the 12-month average yield of the 10-year Singapore Government Securities (SGS) plus 1%, subject to the current floor interest rate of 4% per annum.

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding the use of CPF funds for hospitalisation expenses is CORRECT?

options.text: Funds from CPF Medisave Account can be used for hospitalisation expenses.
Funds from CPF Ordinary, Special Accounts and Retirement Accounts can be used for hospitalisation expenses.
Only funds above $100K from CPF Special Account (SA) can be used for hospitalisation expenses.
50% of CPF funds can be used for hospitalisation expenses.

questions.correct_answer: Funds from CPF Medisave Account can be used for hospitalisation expenses.

questions.question:
Peter, who is from an Asia Pacific country, has recently obtained his permanent residency.
Which of the following regarding his CPF contribution is CORRECT?

options.text: Peter is exempted from CPF contribution as he is a permanent resident and not a citizen.
Peter and his employer are both required to contribute to his CPF.
Peter is only required to contribute to his CPF Medisave account as he is not a Singapore Citizen.
Peter’s contribution to his CPF is voluntary as he is not born in Singapore.

questions.correct_answer: Peter and his employer are both required to contribute to his CPF.
